fix: remove broken startOffset optimization for build discovery

The startOffset calculation assumed Prow build IDs increased by
~80B/day, but they actually increase by ~1.1 quadrillion per
build (~48h interval). This caused jobs with longer intervals
to only discover 3 builds instead of 8.

Simplified to always list all builds via GCS JSON API (single
request for <1000 builds, which covers all CAPZ jobs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: bootstrap React app

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat!: switch from Compliance Policy to Proactive Remediations

BREAKING CHANGE: The solution no longer uses Intune Custom Compliance
Policies. It now uses Proactive Remediations (Device Health Scripts)
which provide:
- Configurable execution frequency (you control the schedule)
- Queryable script output via Graph API (deviceRunStates)
- Optional remediation script (auto-cleans temp files)

Changes:
- Replace ComplianceScripts/ with RemediationScripts/
  - Detect-LowDiskSpace.ps1 (exit 0=OK, 1=low disk)
  - Remediate-LowDiskSpace.ps1 (optional: cleans temp/cache)
- DeviceSyncService now queries deviceHealthScripts/{id}/deviceRunStates
  instead of complianceState, parsing script JSON output
- New config: HealthScriptId, ThresholdGB
- Reduced permissions: removed DeviceManagementManagedDevices.Read.All
- Updated PowerShell sync script and Scheduled Task setup
- Updated README with new architecture and deployment steps

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Schema.org + PROV-O ontology layer for knowledge graph extraction (#63)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement Schema.org + PROV-O ontology-based knowledge graph extraction

- Add backend/config/__init__.py and backend/config/ontology.py with:
  - CORE_ONTOLOGY: 13 Schema.org node types + 26 PROV-O edge types
  - validate_node_type() / validate_edge_type() with 4-level resolution
  - build_validation_report() with compliance percentage metrics
  - generate_ontology_system_prompt() for LLM constraint enforcement
  - LEGACY_NODE_MAP / LEGACY_EDGE_MAP for backward-compatible type remapping

- Add OntologyConstrainedExtractor to entity_extractor.py:
  - Constrains LLM extraction via ontology system prompt
  - Validates/remaps all nodes and edges to canonical Schema.org/PROV-O types
  - Falls back to rule-based extraction when LLM unavailable
  - Returns validation_report with compliance_pct and invalid item details

- Update /extract endpoint in knowledge.py:
  - Add ontology_constrained query parameter (default: true)
  - Use OntologyConstrainedExtractor by default
  - Return validation_report and ontology_constrained flag in response

* Update README.md

* refactor: extract Core and AzureDevOps provider projects (Phase 1 + 2)

Implement the multi-provider architecture by splitting the monolithic
REBUSS.Pure project into a layered solution:

Phase 1 — Extract REBUSS.Pure.Core:
- Create new class library with domain models (PullRequestDiff,
  FullPullRequestMetadata, FileChange, FileContent, etc.)
- Define unified provider interfaces: IScmClient, IPullRequestDataProvider,
  IFileContentDataProvider, IWorkspaceRootProvider
- Add analysis pipeline abstractions: IReviewAnalyzer, AnalysisInput,
  AnalysisSection, ReviewContext, ReviewContextOrchestrator
- Move shared services: IDiffAlgorithm/LcsDiffAlgorithm,
  IStructuredDiffBuilder/StructuredDiffBuilder, IFileClassifier/FileClassifier
- Move custom exceptions to Core/Exceptions

Phase 2 — Extract REBUSS.Pure.AzureDevOps:
- Create new class library with all Azure DevOps-specific code:
  API client, JSON parsers, configuration, authentication chain,
  fine-grained providers, AzureDevOpsScmClient facade
- Add ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddAzureDevOpsProvider() for clean DI
- Update Program.cs to use AddAzureDevOpsProvider(configuration)
  instead of inline registrations
- Make tool handler descriptions provider-agnostic (no "Azure DevOps" strings)
- Metadata handler reads WebUrl/RepositoryFullName from domain model,
  not AzureDevOpsOptions

Cleanup:
- Remove 20+ legacy duplicate files from REBUSS.Pure
- Remove old fine-grained provider interfaces (IPullRequestDiffProvider,
  IPullRequestMetadataProvider, IPullRequestFilesProvider, IFileContentProvider)
- Remove empty legacy directories
- Clean REBUSS.Pure.csproj: remove stale entries and unused packages
- Add InternalsVisibleTo for REBUSS.Pure and REBUSS.Pure.Tests
  on AzureDevOps project

Architecture: Core → AzureDevOps → Pure (host) → Tests
105 files changed, all 486 tests passing.

* Add GitHub PR provider with auto-detection and CLI support

- Introduce REBUSS.Pure.GitHub project: REST API client, config/auth, parsers, providers, and SCM facade
- Dynamic provider selection (GitHub/AzureDevOps) via config, CLI, or git remote auto-detection
- CLI now supports --provider and --owner arguments
- appsettings.json and config system support GitHub section
- Updated docs, solution, and DI registration for new provider
- Added unit tests for all GitHub provider components
- FileContent model now shared by both providers

* Refactor GitHub/Azure auth flows, add CLI/test projects

- Introduce chain-of-responsibility auth for GitHub: PAT → cached token → GitHub CLI → error, with new IGitHubAuthenticationProvider and IGitHubCliTokenProvider
- Add GitHubCliTokenProvider and GitHubCliProcessHelper for cross-platform CLI token acquisition
- Refactor GitHubAuthenticationHandler to use provider, retry on 401/403, and invalidate cache
- Refactor InitCommand to delegate auth to ICliAuthFlow (GitHubCliAuthFlow/AzureDevOpsCliAuthFlow), with provider auto-detection and interactive CLI login/install flows
- Split tests into REBUSS.Pure.Core.Tests, REBUSS.Pure.AzureDevOps.Tests, REBUSS.Pure.GitHub.Tests, and REBUSS.Pure.Tests; update InternalsVisibleTo and references
- Update DI registration, documentation, and .gitignore for new projects and flows
- Add/expand test coverage for all new authentication and CLI logic

* Support dots in GitHub repo names in remote URL parsing

Updated regex in GitHubRemoteDetector to allow repository names with dots for both HTTPS and SSH remote URLs. Added unit tests to ensure correct parsing of such repo names, with and without the .git suffix.

* Add smoke tests for CLI init and MCP protocol flows

- Introduce REBUSS.Pure.SmokeTests project with process-level smoke tests for init (GitHub/Azure DevOps), MCP stdio protocol, and full install flow.
- Add fixtures/helpers: TempGitRepoFixture, CliProcessHelper, McpProcessFixture.
- Update .gitignore for smoke test build output.
- Document smoke test usage in README.
- Enhance CliArgumentParser to support --pat for init command.

* Add live contract tests for Azure DevOps and GitHub MCP

Introduce end-to-end contract tests in REBUSS.Pure.SmokeTests that run the MCP server as a child process and validate its behavior against real Azure DevOps and GitHub fixture PRs. Includes protocol-only tests (no credentials), provider-specific contract tests (metadata, files, diffs, content, negative cases), and shared test infrastructure for process management and response parsing. Tests are auto-skipped if required env vars are missing. Adds CI workflow for contract tests and updates README with usage instructions. No changes to production code—test-only addition.

* Increase timeouts and refactor CI env var handling

Increase default timeouts in smoke tests for better reliability in slow environments. Update contract-tests.yml to add REBUSS.Pure.SmokeTests to the path filter and move environment variable definitions to the job level, removing redundant per-step env blocks.

* Update test docs, fixture expectations, and solution config

- Move test instructions from README.md to new DeveloperGuide.md
- Update AdoTestExpectations to match actual fixture PR details
- Relax contract test assertions to require only additions > 0
- Bump Visual Studio version in solution to 18.x
- Add DeveloperGuide.md to Solution Items
- Remove REBUSS.Pure.SmokeTests project from solution

* Update CI for Node 24, refine build/tests, and clean code

- Set FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24 in CI configs to enforce Node.js 24 for JavaScript actions
- Limit contract-tests.yml build step to REBUSS.Pure.SmokeTests for efficiency
- Remove unnecessary null checks in commit hash logging
- Use Assert.Single for clearer test assertions in InitCommandTests
- Apply null-forgiving operator to test assertions for Required fields

* Update file diff tests and expected file status values

Adjusted file path assertion to ignore leading slashes in AdoFileDiffContractTests for robustness. Updated expected status of "src/Calculator.cs" from "edit" to "modified" in AdoTestExpectations to align with API terminology.

* Fixed failing test

* Fixed contract tests

* contract test fixes

* amended contract test

* Improve CLI tool hiding in smoke tests; better diagnostics

Enhance BuildRestrictedPathEnv to robustly hide gh/az: on Windows, filter PATH more strictly; on Linux/macOS, prepend shadow scripts to PATH. Update XML docs to clarify platform-specific logic. In FullInstallSmokeTests, include stdout/stderr in failure and timeout messages for improved troubleshooting.

* Optimize smoke test process startup and error handling

Use compile-time build config and --no-build for dotnet run in test helpers to avoid redundant builds. Add robust error handling for stdin/stdout pipe races and process exit timing, improving test speed and reliability.

* Improve robustness of FullInstallSmokeTests process handling

- Use compile-time build config and --no-build for dotnet pack to avoid MSBuild/VBCSCompiler pipe handle issues in parallel test runs
- Add 60s timeout to init command to prevent hangs
- Wrap all process stdin writes/closes in try/catch (IOException) to handle early process exits
- Handle OperationCanceledException on pipe reads; force-kill process and return empty output if pipes are held open by child processes
- Include timeout duration in error messages for better diagnostics
- Align process handling with patterns used in CliProcessHelper for consistency and reliability

* Refactor process helpers to avoid WaitForExitAsync hangs

Replace WaitForExitAsync with WaitForExit(timeout) in smoke test helpers to prevent hangs when child processes inherit pipe handles. Pipe reads now use a separate CancellationTokenSource and a post-exit grace period to ensure output is drained without blocking indefinitely. Improves reliability of test infrastructure when running dotnet, az, or gh shims.

* Harden full install smoke test against CI CLI interference

Restrict PATH during init to shadow az/gh CLI tools with dummy scripts, preventing hangs from interactive az login on CI runners. RunProcessAsync now accepts environment overrides to support this. Improves test reliability in environments with pre-installed CLIs.

* Exclude ContractAdo and ContractGitHub tests in CI/CD

Updated ci.yml, publish.yml, and release.yml to filter out tests with Category=ContractAdo and Category=ContractGitHub during dotnet test runs. This ensures these contract tests are not executed as part of the standard CI, publish, or release workflows.

* Hide create-pr.md prompt for the end user, because it's not ready for deploy

The create-pr.md Copilot prompt is no longer copied to .github/prompts/ by the rebuss-pure init command; it remains in embedded resources for future use. Documentation and tests are updated to reflect that only review-pr.md and self-review.md are deployed. The #create-pr command and prompt are not yet available to users.

* Update config precedence: detected > cached for ADO/GitHub

Change config resolution to prefer detected repo info over cached.
Update docs and tests to match new precedence (user > detected > cached).
Add comprehensive tests for GitHubConfigurationResolver, including
workspace root integration and all fallback scenarios.

* Always overwrite prompts/instructions on init; add instructions dir

Init now always copies prompt and instruction files from embedded resources to .github/prompts/ and .github/instructions/, overwriting any existing files to ensure updates are deployed. Adds .github/instructions/ for Copilot custom instructions. Updates tests and docs to reflect new behavior and directory. File copying occurs before authentication, so files are created even if auth fails. Contributors should only edit embedded source files.

* Improve GitHub PR parsing, CLI token expiry, and config routing

- Distinguish merged vs. non-merged closed PRs ("completed" vs. "abandoned") in PR parser and tests
- Add ParseWithCommits for efficient PR metadata and SHA extraction
- Optimize diff provider: single-parse PR metadata, parallel file content fetch
- GitHubApiClient: only return null for 404, throw for other errors
- Auth handler: avoid retry on rate-limit 403 (X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0)
- Reduce GitHub CLI token cache lifetime from 365 days to 24 hours
- Route PATs and repo config to correct provider section based on CLI args; normalize provider names
- Update comments for clarity and fix encoding issues

* Add CancellationToken support to GitHub API client

All IGitHubApiClient methods now accept an optional CancellationToken, and the implementation passes it to all HttpClient calls. Internal helpers and all consumers (diff, file content, metadata providers) are updated to propagate cancellation. GitHubCliTokenProvider now uses a linked timeout token for process output reading. This enables prompt cancellation of network and process operations, improving responsiveness and reliability.

fix(uniflight): eliminate race condition in panic propagation tests (#342)

Replace tokio::spawn + sleep-based synchronization with eager cell
registration via sequential execute() calls and tokio::join!.

The original tests relied on sleep(10ms) to ensure the follower task was
polled before the leader completed. In tokio's single-threaded runtime,
the leader could finish and clean up the DashMap entry before the
follower task was ever polled, causing the follower to become a new
leader and return Ok instead of Err(LeaderPanicked).

The fix exploits the fact that execute() eagerly calls
get_or_create_cell() before returning the future. Creating both futures
sequentially guarantees the follower joins the leader's cell
deterministically, with no timing dependency.

Co-authored-by: Kateřina Churanová <katerina.churanova@microsoft.com>
